ddls:
CREATE TABLE "transmitters" (
  "transmitter_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each transmitter */,
  "transmitter_name" TEXT /* Name of the transmitter */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the transmitter's function */,
  "manufacturer" TEXT /* Manufacturer of the transmitter */,
  "model" TEXT /* Model number of the transmitter */,
  "serial_number" TEXT /* Serial number of the transmitter */,
  "date_of_last_calibration" TEXT /* Date of the last calibration */,
  "location" TEXT /* Physical location of the transmitter */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("transmitter_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "receivers" (
  "receiver_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each receiver */,
  "receiver_name" TEXT /* Name of the receiver */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the receiver's function */,
  "manufacturer" TEXT /* Manufacturer of the receiver */,
  "model" TEXT /* Model number of the receiver */,
  "serial_number" TEXT /* Serial number of the receiver */,
  "date_of_last_calibration" TEXT /* Date of the last calibration */,
  "location" TEXT /* Physical location of the receiver */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("receiver_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "fields" (
  "field_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each field */,
  "field_name" TEXT /* Name of the field */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the field's purpose in the configuration */,
  "data_type" TEXT /* Data type of the field */,
  "default_value" TEXT /* Default value of the field */,
  "is_required" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the field is required */,
  "is_configurable" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the field can be modified */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("field_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "configurations" (
  "config_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each configuration */,
  "transmitter_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the transmitter */,
  "receiver_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the receiver */,
  "field_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the field */,
  "length" TEXT /* Maximum length of the value */,
  "value" TEXT /* Actual value of the configuration */,
  "comment" TEXT /* Additional comments about the configuration */,
  "bit_order" TEXT /* Bit order (e.g., MSB, LSB) */,
  "synchronization_type" TEXT /* Synchronization type (e.g., synchronous, asynchronous) */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the configuration is active */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who created the configuration */,
  "created_date" TEXT /* Date when the configuration was created */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("config_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_configurations_field_id FOREIGN KEY ("field_id") REFERENCES fields ("field_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_configurations_receiver_id FOREIGN KEY ("receiver_id") REFERENCES receivers ("receiver_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_configurations_transmitter_id FOREIGN KEY ("transmitter_id") REFERENCES transmitters ("transmitter_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_configurations_created_by FOREIGN KEY ("created_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "config_history" (
  "history_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each history entry */,
  "config_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the configuration */,
  "change_date" TEXT /* Date when the change was made */,
  "previous_value" TEXT /* Previous value before the change */,
  "changed_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who made the change */,
  "reason_for_change" TEXT /* Reason for the change */,
  "is_reverted" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the change has been reverted */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("history_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_config_history_changed_by FOREIGN KEY ("changed_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_config_history_config_id FOREIGN KEY ("config_id") REFERENCES configurations ("config_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., engineer, admin) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department of the user */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the user account is active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alerts" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert */,
  "config_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the configuration */,
  "alert_date" TEXT /* Date when the alert was generated */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the alert */,
  "severity" TEXT /* Severity of the alert (e.g., low, medium, high) */,
  "resolved_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who resolved the alert */,
  "resolved_date" TEXT /* Date when the alert was resolved */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_resolved_by FOREIGN KEY ("resolved_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_config_id FOREIGN KEY ("config_id") REFERENCES configurations ("config_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each report */,
  "config_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the configuration */,
  "report_date" TEXT /* Date when the report was generated */,
  "summary" TEXT /* Summary of the report */,
  "report_type" TEXT /* Type of the report (e.g., configuration summary, performance report) */,
  "generated_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who generated the report */,
  "file_path" TEXT /* File path to the generated report */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reports_config_id FOREIGN KEY ("config_id") REFERENCES configurations ("config_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reports_generated_by FOREIGN KEY ("generated_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "versions" (
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each version */,
  "config_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the configuration */,
  "version_number" INTEGER /* Version number of the configuration */,
  "version_date" TEXT /* Date when the version was created */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the changes made in this version */,
  "reverted_from" INTEGER /* Version this version was reverted from, if applicable */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_versions_config_id FOREIGN KEY ("config_id") REFERENCES configurations ("config_id")
)
